purpose: Finite polygon-chord fixture spec for moving surfaceology checks from arbitrary graph abstraction toward explicit curve-intersection data. The checker enumerates internal chords of convex n-gons for n=4..7, derives chord crossing graphs by endpoint interleaving, and checks binary witnesses induced by maximal noncrossing chord sets.

generator_scope.curve_model: internal chords in a convex polygon, used only as a finite curve-intersection fixture abstraction
generator_scope.crossing_rule: two chords cross when their endpoints strictly interleave around the polygon
generator_scope.witness_rule: maximal noncrossing chord sets are maximal independent sets in the chord-crossing graph

equation_template: u_chord + product_{crossing chords} u_crossing = 1, with empty product equal to 1.
required_features: all checked polygons have at least one crossing edge
n=6 and n=7 include chords crossing at least four other chords
n=7 has more than ten internal chords
every maximal noncrossing witness satisfies every generated u-equation
non_promotion_boundary: This polygon-chord fixture family checks only finite convex-polygon chord intersections for n=4..7. It is not a surfaceology theorem, not full source-faithful surface curve enumeration, not a general positive-region solver, not Formation Medium substrate evidence, not a physical settling law, and not engineering readiness.
